question: Will China invade Taiwan before GTA VI?
narrative_context: US intelligence (March 2026): Beijing prefers non-military unification, invasion unlikely
Eurasia Group (late 2025): Military purges defer invasion option ≥2 years (through ~April 2028)
US assessment: Amphibious assault extremely difficult with high failure risk
ASPI: '2026 is not the year' for Taiwan invasion; Beijing prioritizes economic coercion
evidence: US intelligence (March 2026): Beijing prefers non-military unification; invasion unlikely
Eurasia Group (late 2025): Military purges defer invasion ≥2 years (through ~April 2028)
US assessment: Amphibious assault 'extremely difficult' with high failure risk
ASPI: '2026 is not the year' for Taiwan invasion (April 2026)
counter_evidence: Some analysts argue China could exploit Taiwan's weaknesses with surprise invasion, but US intelligence explicitly rules out 2026.
thesis: This is a 41.5pp mispricing. US intelligence explicitly rules out Taiwan invasion in 2026. Military constraints and structural deterrents push against military action by June 2026. Market prices 51.5% when experts assess 5–15%.
x_thread: China invades Taiwan by June 2026? Market: 51.5% YES. US intel: 10% at most. That's a 41.5pp gap.

US intelligence (March 2026): Beijing prefers non-military unification. Eurasia Group: Military purges defer invasion until ≥2028. ASPI: '2026 is not the year.' Structural constraints prevent military action.

Markets misprice geopolitics when ignoring constraints. Taiwan's defenses + China's military readiness = invasion risky. US intel is explicit: not 2026.
